content.problemStatement:
Chef has closed his restaurant and decided to run a fruit stand instead. His signature dish is a fruit chaat consisting of 222 bananas and 111 apple. He currently has XXX bananas and YYY apples. How many chaats can he make with the fruits he currently has?


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase consists of a single line containing two space separated integers - XXX and YYY


Output Format
For each testcase, output the answer on a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the number of bananas and apples Chef currently has.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line: the maximum number of fruit chaats Chef can prepare.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 72 bananas give 36 possible chaats (2 per chaat) and there are 50 apples, so the limiting factor is bananas → 36 chaats.
Test case 2: 38 bananas allow 19 chaats, apples are abundant (93), so answer is 19.
Test case 3: 51 bananas allow 25 chaats but only 4 apples are available, thus only 4 chaats can be made.

First case: no fruits → 0 chaats.
Second case: 5 bananas give at most 2 chaats, apples are 2, so 2 chaats can be prepared.
7 bananas allow 3 chaats (floor(7/2)=3) and there are exactly 3 apples, so 3 chaats can be made.

solution.approach: Each chaat requires exactly 2 bananas and 1 apple. For a given stock (X bananas, Y apples) the number of chaats is limited by the bananas (X/2) and by the apples (Y). The answer is the minimum of these two values, using integer division for the bananas. Process each test case independently in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        out_lines.append(str(min(X // 2, Y)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
